title: The Baby Show

jokes.text: Hey, this isn't gonna change the way you dress or eat lollipops, is it?
jokes.setup: Character congratulating Cerie on engagement
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific concern about engagement affecting lollipop consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific absurdism. Only Tracy would have this concern, and the specificity of 'lollipops' makes it unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: Area code 407? Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Jack sees phone number on caller ID
jokes.punchline: Immediate dread at specific area code suggesting problem caller
jokes.explanation: Efficient setup for Jack's mother issues - area code as immediate threat indicator

jokes.text: Jonathan, these cheap phones keep on shattering!
jokes.setup: Jack is stressed about mysterious 407 area code calls
jokes.punchline: Visual of Jack apparently breaking phones in frustration
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy beat showing Jack's stress through property destruction.

jokes.text: Who let 407 through? Jonathan, we have drills for this!
jokes.setup: Jack treating phone calls like a security threat
jokes.punchline: Revelation that they have emergency drills for specific area codes
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity perfectly. The idea of phone call drills is both ridiculous and perfectly in character for corporate Jack.

jokes.text: Business doesn't get me down. Business gets me off.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if business has Jack down
jokes.punchline: Jack's sexual relationship with business success
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work. Classic Jack line that's both quotable and reveals his priorities.

jokes.text: Lemon, do you realize that your little show accounts for 3% of our revenue but takes up 90% of my time?
jokes.setup: Liz bothering Jack about jackets
jokes.punchline: Absurd mathematical breakdown of TGS's cost-benefit ratio
jokes.explanation: Good workplace comedy - specific numbers make it feel real while highlighting the show-within-show dynamic.

jokes.text: We must get our freak on while our fruit is a-plentiful.
jokes.setup: Tracy talking about partying
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-biblical/poetic language about getting freaky
jokes.explanation: Tracy's unique voice - mixing high and low register language in a way only he would.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Liz Lemon! I like to wear man shirts. Watch me skateboard.
jokes.setup: Tracy doing an impression of Liz to show how hurtful impressions are
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific and somewhat accurate character details
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Tracy's impression captures something true about Liz while being absurd.

jokes.text: I don't skateboard.
jokes.setup: Tracy's impression includes skateboarding
jokes.punchline: Liz's deadpan correction of the least important detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz response - missing the point and focusing on a minor factual error.

jokes.text: Tracy, stop tasering him.
jokes.setup: Argument between Tracy and Josh
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Tracy using a taser in the workplace
jokes.explanation: Throwaway line that reveals the absurd workplace violence that's apparently normal at TGS.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry, my babysitter got jury duty. I think I'm going to kill myself.
jokes.setup: Cerie explaining why she can't do something
jokes.punchline: Casual suicidal ideation over minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cerie - young person's tendency to dramatize minor problems taken to absurd extreme.

jokes.text: He does this thing where he screams at limo drivers.
jokes.setup: Cerie describing why her fiancé is funny
jokes.punchline: Thinking abuse of service workers is endearing humor
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Cerie's skewed values and privilege.

jokes.text: But we both wanna have babies while it's still cool.
jokes.setup: Cerie justifying rushing into marriage
jokes.punchline: Treating having babies like a fashion trend
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures millennial mindset about life decisions being trend-driven.

jokes.text: If it's a girl, Bookcase. Or Sandstorm. Or maybe Hat. But that's more of a boy's name.
jokes.setup: Cerie sharing baby names she's picked
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous object names, with gender distinctions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurd baby names with the perfect kicker that 'Hat' is masculine. Pure 30 Rock absurdism.

jokes.text: Like having a career. And working. And...having a job. And working.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to convince Cerie there are other things besides babies
jokes.punchline: Liz running out of distinct things to say, repeating herself
jokes.explanation: Good Liz moment - her awkwardness when trying to be inspiring shows through repetitive phrasing.

jokes.text: You only have a really short period where you can be a young, hot mom. If you wait too long, you could be like...50 at your kid's graduation.
jokes.setup: Cerie explaining urgency about having babies
jokes.punchline: Treating 50 as ancient, setting up Liz's age insecurity
jokes.explanation: Sets up the age-based cringe but isn't particularly original or surprising.

jokes.text: 50's not that old, Cerie.
jokes.setup: Liz defending 50 as not old
jokes.punchline: Walking into Cerie's trap about her age
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the age reveal punchline that follows.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Are you 50 now?
jokes.setup: Previous exchange about 50 not being old
jokes.punchline: Cerie innocently revealing Liz's age insecurity
jokes.explanation: Well-executed age comedy setup/payoff. The innocent delivery makes it sting more.

jokes.text: Because I enjoy his salad dressings and lemonade.
jokes.setup: Kenneth choosing Paul Newman over Robert Redford
jokes.punchline: Basing choice on food products rather than acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth logic - completely missing the point of the hypothetical in a sweetly naive way.

jokes.text: What if my junk goes bad?
jokes.setup: Liz panicking about biological clock
jokes.punchline: Crude terminology for reproductive organs
jokes.explanation: Liz's awkward way of discussing fertility fears. Economy is good but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Wow, red wine is not your drink.
jokes.setup: Liz having emotional breakdown about babies
jokes.punchline: Jenna's shallow response focusing on alcohol tolerance
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna moment - completely missing the emotional content and focusing on superficial details.

jokes.text: Like that hot dancer Madonna used to have Lourdes.
jokes.setup: Jenna offering to help Liz get a guy
jokes.punchline: Confusing Carlos Leon with Jesus at Lourdes
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna malapropism mixing pop culture with religious references in a characteristically confused way.

jokes.text: Go to hell.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's cheerful 'Good morning, Mr. Donaghy!'
jokes.punchline: Jack's hostile response to pleasantries
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast showing Jack's mood. Economy is perfect but not very original.

jokes.text: Or being chained to a wall in someone's sex dungeon.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing examples of people having worse days
jokes.punchline: Escalating from bee stings to extreme sexual captivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from innocent to disturbing delivered with Kenneth's characteristic sweetness. The contrast makes it funnier.

jokes.text: But to that woman... I'm always gonna be the punk kid who cried when Pop was run over by a mail truck.
jokes.setup: Jack talking about his mother seeing him as weak
jokes.punchline: Setup for the reveal that Pop was his dog, not his father
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the payoff. Creates sympathy before the reveal that subverts it.

jokes.text: No, Pop was my dog. My dad left when I was two... so I grew up calling my Collie Pop.
jokes.setup: Previous setup about Pop being run over
jokes.punchline: Pop was a dog, plus casual mention of abandonment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff that also reveals character background. The casual mention of abandonment adds another layer.

jokes.text: My mother tried to send me to Vietnam to make a man out of me. I was 12.
jokes.setup: Comparing mothers after Kenneth talks about his
jokes.punchline: Absurdly inappropriate parenting and impossible timing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of bad parenting. The age detail makes it both impossible and funnier. Classic Jack backstory.

jokes.text: Mama, you carried me for nine months. Let me carry you now.
jokes.setup: Kenneth sharing what he tells his mother
jokes.punchline: Sweet sentiment that Jack will later steal
jokes.explanation: Sweet Kenneth moment that becomes funnier when Jack tries to use it. Good character work and setup.

jokes.text: With a tea garden and fake rocks made of foam because she falls down a lot.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his mother's retirement home
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of frequent falls requiring safety measures
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aging delivered matter-of-factly. The foam rocks detail is nicely specific.

jokes.text: I'm also listed under meth addiction and child psychiatry.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman explaining his listing in health manual
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate medical specialties
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman introduction - immediately establishes his questionable credentials in characteristic style.

jokes.text: I should start by saying that I can't personally help you conceive. Something happened to me while scuba diving.
jokes.setup: Doctor offering fertility consultation
jokes.punchline: Mysterious diving-related fertility issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Spaceman moment. The vague reference to a diving incident is perfectly mysterious and inappropriate.

jokes.text: 'Cause you haven't mentioned it in like three hours.
jokes.setup: Writer mentioning Harvard as qualification
jokes.punchline: Liz pointing out his constant Harvard name-dropping
jokes.explanation: Decent workplace comedy about annoying colleague behavior. Not particularly original but well-delivered.

jokes.text: But I do like to get yelled at during sex.
jokes.setup: Writer listing qualifications as sperm donor
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual preference overshare
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from oversharing but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: No, I just told my wife I did.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Josh had a vasectomy
jokes.punchline: Revealing he lied to his wife about sterilization
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about marital deception. Good economy and reveals character moral flexibility.

jokes.text: I fake it.
jokes.setup: Liz asking how his wife doesn't get pregnant
jokes.punchline: Male version of faking orgasm taken literally
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of typical 'faking it' premise with absurd practical application. Great economy.

jokes.text: I have a small ferret farm about 60 miles north of the city.
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering to father Liz's child
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and unusual farming operation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth detail - unexpected, specific, and somehow both sweet and creepy. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I can get cable if you want, 'cause I know how much you like the TV.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing amenities of ferret farm
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing about TV watching
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's formal, awkward way of describing normal activities. 'The TV' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Sometimes I watch you watching it.
jokes.setup: Kenneth being helpful about TV
jokes.punchline: Admission of creepy stalking behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from sweet to creepy. Kenneth's innocent delivery makes it funnier and more unsettling.

jokes.text: Oprah and Steadman are the same person.
jokes.setup: Tracy sharing conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Impossible identity theory about famous couple
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy conspiracy theory with perfect economy. Absurd but delivered with conviction.

jokes.text: That's why he/she won't run for president.
jokes.setup: Tracy's Oprah/Steadman theory
jokes.punchline: Following the logic to explain Oprah's political choices
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Tracy thinking through his absurd theory logically.

jokes.text: The Mars probe didn't brake. It was attacked by a Martian cougar.
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation for space mission failure
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism with specific detail about Martian fauna. Stays in character.

jokes.text: This is untoward. This is not toward!
jokes.setup: Tracy expressing anger about the situation
jokes.punchline: Creating opposite of 'untoward' that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Tracy's attempt at sophisticated vocabulary backfiring in a characteristic way.

jokes.text: And then he's gonna fold us up in a pizza and eat us.
jokes.setup: Josh describing what Jack will do when angry
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and impossible method of murder/cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd escalation. The specificity of being folded into pizza makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: But do you still want a sound hook-up so you can listen to rehearsal?
jokes.setup: Liz's outburst about not wanting sexual hook-ups
jokes.punchline: Technical meaning of 'hook-up' in audio context
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on double meaning of 'hook-up.' Workplace comedy that deflates tension.

jokes.text: You see, Josh's father's an assassin with the Russian mafia. They call him El Matador.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he can't fire Josh
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous backstory mixing cultures inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist lie with cultural mixing that makes no sense. El Matador for Russian mafia is a nice touch.

jokes.text: I'm sending you our new super top secret invisible motorcycle.
jokes.setup: Jack rewarding Tracy for being understanding
jokes.punchline: Impossible technology as reward
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag while showing Jack's willingness to lie outrageously to manage Tracy.

jokes.text: For realses.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking if invisible motorcycle is real
jokes.punchline: Jack using Tracy-style language to convince him
jokes.explanation: Jack code-switching to Tracy's speech patterns to be more convincing. Good character interaction.

jokes.text: I keep pooping during sex.
jokes.setup: Jack needing to see a doctor
jokes.punchline: Extremely embarrassing medical condition
jokes.explanation: Shocking admission that's both funny and sets up later callback. Jack's vulnerability makes it work.

jokes.text: Don't put little notes in their lunch bag that say, 'Mommy's watching you.' People find those things.
jokes.setup: Jack giving single parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Creepy maternal surveillance disguised as affection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about helicopter parenting taken to stalker levels. Very quotable and specific.

jokes.text: This is a woman that actually had a heart attack to prevent me from going on my honeymoon.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his mother's manipulative nature
jokes.punchline: Extreme medical manipulation for control
jokes.explanation: Escalates mother manipulation to absurd medical extremes. Perfect Jack family dysfunction.

jokes.text: You carried me for nine months. Let me carry you now.
jokes.setup: Jack trying Kenneth's line with his mother
jokes.punchline: Stealing Kenneth's sweet sentiment word-for-word
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jack desperately trying Kenneth's approach. Works because it's so unlike Jack.

jokes.text: Well, right back at you, Colleen! Yeah, that's right! You cut Pop's balls off! And left him in the street to die!
jokes.setup: Jack's attempt at being nice backfiring
jokes.punchline: Explosive revelation about dog castration and abandonment
jokes.explanation: Explosive escalation that callbacks to Pop the dog with shocking new details. Peak Jack family dysfunction.

jokes.text: Russian mobs, invisible motorcycles... Sex pooping.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his call with Jack
jokes.punchline: Listing Jack's lies with casual mention of embarrassing condition
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure listing Jack's various issues. 'Sex pooping' as casual mention is funny.

jokes.text: If in the future... In the future... If I mention anything crazy... Like an invisible motorcycle... It means I'm off my meds... and should be ignored.
jokes.setup: Josh impersonating Jack to fix the situation
jokes.punchline: Creating excuse for all of Jack's lies
jokes.explanation: Clever solution to the lying problem that also mocks the setup. Meta humor about the situation.

jokes.text: I just wanted to sound smart.
jokes.setup: Jenna listing the Koran as favorite book
jokes.punchline: Admitting to fake intellectualism
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna shallowness but not particularly original or surprising.

jokes.text: Who's the cutest baby in the world?
jokes.setup: Liz reluctantly holding a baby
jokes.punchline: Immediately falling into baby talk
jokes.explanation: Predictable baby interaction comedy. Shows Liz's maternal instincts but not original.

jokes.text: I just bought the moon.
jokes.setup: Jack having breakdown and bragging
jokes.punchline: Impossible purchase claim
jokes.explanation: Peak Jack grandiosity taken to absurd extreme. Perfect character moment of breakdown.

jokes.text: And she will outlive you. She's like Castro.
jokes.setup: Jack talking about daily calls from his mother
jokes.punchline: Comparing mother's longevity to dictator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comparison mixing family dynamics with political commentary. Very quotable and dark.

jokes.text: Oh, right right. And you have someone else's baby in your car.
jokes.setup: Liz's highway hypnosis comparison
jokes.punchline: Extending analogy to highlight absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that highlights the absurdity of Liz's comparison. Great straight-man work.

jokes.text: Well, Anna calls her Isobel, but I call her Nancy.
jokes.setup: Jack asking baby's name
jokes.punchline: Liz has renamed someone else's baby
jokes.explanation: Escalates the kidnapping by showing Liz has psychologically adopted the baby. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, yes. I'm a big fan of kidnapping. Especially by my middle management.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Jack is enjoying this situation
jokes.punchline: Deadpan sarcasm about enjoying employee criminality
jokes.explanation: Good Jack deadpan delivery highlighting the absurdity of workplace kidnapping.

jokes.text: I was talking about you at work today, Mother. I think my boss, Mr. Jack Donaghy, wishes you were his mother.
jokes.setup: Kenneth talking to his mother
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong assessment of Jack's mother situation
jokes.explanation: Good irony given Jack's mother problems. Kenneth's innocent misreading of the situation.

jokes.text: It's just like Charlie's Angels!
jokes.setup: Kenneth confirming speaker phone use
jokes.punchline: Comparing family call to action TV show
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent pop culture reference that makes little sense. In character but mild.

jokes.text: I'm looking at the kooky skeleton right now. I love it! You're the best mother in the whole world!
jokes.setup: Kenneth thanking his mother for decorations
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top enthusiasm for Halloween skeleton
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment showing his enthusiasm but not particularly memorable.

tmdb_overview: When ditzy receptionist Cerie spreads news of her engagement to a filthy rich Greek heir, Liz confronts her own marital and maternal status. Meanwhile, emotionally hardened Jack softens due to "mommy issues."
insight: Network television finally lets Liz Lemon breathe with 64 jokes in 23 minutes.
